svg.text: Weaviate API — Lab Cheatsheet
Connect → Configure → Insert → Query: every code pattern from C1M3 Ungraded Lab 1
1. Setup — connect to embedded Weaviate

text.tspan: # ... and remember to client.close() at the end

svg.text: 2. Configure the database — collection + named vectorizer + properties
2a) Vectorizer config
2b) Create the collection
Key concepts to remember
Collection = group of indexed objects (Weaviate's "table").
NamedVectors = one collection can hold multiple vectorizations.
source_properties = which fields get concatenated & embedded.
vectorize_property_name=True → the property name is prefixed
before its value when embedding (helps disambiguate values
like "Moderate" by giving them the context "budget: Moderate").
DataType options used: TEXT, NUMBER, DATE.
Not every property needs vectorization — numbers and dates
are typically left structured for filtering, not search.
reranker_config attaches a cross-encoder reranker module.
Re-creating an existing collection raises an exception — always
guard with client.collections.exists(name) first.
List all collections: client.collections.list_all().keys()
3. Insert data — batch + UUID for idempotency

text.tspan: # What happens behind the scenes for every add_object():
# 1. source_properties are concatenated and embedded via text2vec-transformers
# 2. The vector is inserted into the HNSW graph (incremental index update)
# 3. Inverted index + filter index updated for BM25 / metadata queries

svg.text: 4. Filters — strict, structured pre-filter

text.tspan: # Pass via `filters=...` to any query. Filters run BEFORE scoring → cheap.

svg.text: 5. Four ways to query the collection

text.tspan: 'I want suggestions to travel during Winter...'

g.text: Embeds your query → HNSW returns vectors closest by cosine.
Filters narrow the candidate set before the vector scan.
Pure lexical scoring (BM25 on the inverted index).
No embedding step → very fast, but blind to synonyms.
Same args as near_text: query, filters, limit.
alpha = 0 → pure BM25 | alpha = 1 → pure vector
Under the hood: runs both, merges with Reciprocal Rank Fusion.
Good default: alpha=0.5; tune per dataset.
Second-stage cross-encoder reorders the top-k from any search.
Works with near_text, bm25, hybrid — just add the rerank= arg.

svg.text: 6. Result object & closing the client

text.tspan: # Always release resources at the end:

svg.text: Things to remember for the assignment
✓ Guard every collections.create() with .exists() first
✓ Use generate_uuid5(doc) for deterministic, dedupable IDs
✓ Pick source_properties carefully — only what's worth embedding
✓ Numeric / date fields → keep them as NUMBER/DATE for filtering
✓ Every query supports query, filters, limit — same shape
✓ Reranker is a drop-in rerank= kwarg, works on all four modes
